beats.text: Four signal waveforms — s-one, s-two, s-three, s-four — appear on a time axis.
Gram-Schmidt starts with s-one. An arrow singles it out from the group.
To build the first basis function, we need s-one's energy. The integral of s-one squared over time lights up — that's E-one.
Now divide. Psi-one of t equals s-one of t divided by the square root of E-one. Watch the waveform rescale until its energy hits exactly one.
That's psi-one — the first orthonormal basis function. Unit energy, locked in. Every other basis function will be built perpendicular to this one.
